notes: Roman emperor (r. 363-364), former chief officer of the guards, noted for his Christian piety and brief reign after Julian the Apostate.

docs.title: BOOK XXV
docs.author: Ammianus Marcellinus
docs.excerpt: Jovian was slow in his movements, of a cheerful countenance, with blue eyes; very tall, so much so that it was long before any of the royal robes could be found to fit him.

docs.title: Letter 56
docs.author: Athanasius of Alexandria
docs.subject_summary: Copy of a letter of the Emperor Jovian, sent to Athanasius, the most holy Archbishop of Alexandria:
docs.excerpt: … for heavenly things is suitable to a religious Emperor; for thus you will truly have 'your heart' also 'in the hand of God.' Since then your Piety desired to learn from us the faith of the Catholic Church, giving thanks for these things to the Lord, we counselled above all things to remind your
